module_name: Micks Picks World Cup Path Leverage Upgrade
purpose: Add World Cup bracket-path, group-finish, and home-field leverage rules so soccer futures and group-stage markets are graded by path value instead of raw team strength only.
core_rule: Do not bet World Cup teams only because they are stronger. Evaluate whether the market price underrates how group finish changes the knockout path.

major_concepts.conditional_group_finish_value: Cup Win % from the Silver Bulletin screenshots is conditional on group finish. Use it to compare path value of 1st vs 2nd vs 3rd, not as raw pre-tournament title probability.
major_concepts.group_winner_leverage: If a team's Cup Win % nearly doubles or triples when finishing 1st, prioritize group winner and live group-position markets.
major_concepts.third_place_warning: Third-place advancement may be possible, but the path is usually worse and should be downgraded unless price compensates.
major_concepts.host_field_adjustment: USA, Mexico, and Canada should not be treated as neutral-field teams when playing at home.
major_concepts.bracket_collision_risk: Spain Group H and Argentina Group J have important path collision risk if one wins its group while the other finishes second.

Argentina.micks_read: High-priority group winner and live group-position market.

Spain.micks_read: High-priority group winner and live group-position market.

France.micks_read: Group I winner market is high priority. Monitor Senegal/Norway disruption risk.

England.micks_read: Strong team, but path sensitive and may involve host-field problems. Do not overpay futures.

Germany.micks_read: Medium-high priority group winner and live futures team.

Brazil.micks_read: Solid group-winner interest, but less urgent than Argentina/Spain/France.

Norway.micks_read: Group I leverage team. Monitor vs France/Senegal.

Senegal.micks_read: Important Group I disruptor. Qualify/top-2/upset markets can create value.

group_stage_game_leverage_watchlist.reason: Highest bracket leverage; Spain finishing 2nd can trigger Argentina collision risk.
Group I leverage; affects France, Norway, Senegal path.
Early Argentina group shock could reshape Spain/Argentina bracket.
Group I disruption and top-2/third-place implications.
England group path and host-field bracket complications.
France group positioning and Group I knockout path.
Colombia/Portugal bracket leverage and group winner value.
Critical Group I qualify/top-2 market leverage.
Brazil group control and Morocco disruption test.

betting_market_priority_order: Group winner markets for Spain, Argentina, France, Germany, Colombia, Brazil
Top-2 / to qualify from group markets for Senegal, Norway, USA, Mexico, Canada, Colombia
Live group-position markets after first match
Knockout to-advance markets involving hosts at home
World Cup outright only after path improves or price overreacts
Friendly moneylines only when there is independent price edge

minimum_requirements: Market price must be compared to path-adjusted probability, not just raw team rating.
At least 3 independent supports: model/path leverage, price edge, opponent/path weakness, home-field or schedule advantage, or market mispricing.
For group winner bets, finishing 1st must materially improve title or knockout path value.
For host-country bets, home-field venue must be confirmed and market must not already fully price it.
For heavy favorites, A grade is forbidden unless price still creates clear value.
automatic_caps: Heavy friendly moneylines capped at C/B- unless price is clearly wrong.
Outrights capped at B unless path and price both improve.
Third-place qualification paths capped unless odds compensate for tougher bracket.
Games with expected heavy rotation capped unless lineup confirms.
